settings.title: What a Day in the Pipeworks Van Actually Looks Like
settings.excerpt: No two jobs run the same, and the tidy ones are rarely the ones that stick in the memory.
settings.body:
People picture plumbing as one big dramatic burst. Most days are quieter and stranger than that.

A recent Tuesday for our engineer Dec started with a dripping radiator valve, moved to a child's toy wedged firmly in a toilet trap, and ended with an elderly customer whose only real problem was a tap washer worn paper thin.

The toy job took the longest. The tap took four minutes and a fifty-pence part, and got the warmest thank you of the day.

We carry common spares on every van precisely because of days like that. The small fix done on the spot beats a second visit every time.
